codeReview.summary: The auth-email-otp feature implementation is complete and correct. All expectedBehavior items are implemented: email/password registration with password strength validation, login with rate limiting, logout with server-side token invalidation, OTP request/verify with mock SMS, JWT access/refresh token rotation with reuse detection. The implementation follows the architecture patterns defined in AGENTS.md and uses the repository/service/router structure.

issues.description: The get_current_user function has unused db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db parameter that is never used. This is dead code that should be removed.

RateLimiter uses in-memory dict (self.attempts) which will not work correctly in multi-instance deployments or after application restart. For production, consider using Redis-based rate limiting as documented in AGENTS.md caching strategy.

User ID is generated with uuid.uuid4() instead of relying on database default. This is not incorrect but could be simplified by removing the explicit id parameter since the model has default=uuid.uuid4.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker reported test file (test_auth.py) was not committed due to Droid-Shield false positive on test passwords. The test file now exists in the codebase but the original commit (d87755b) did not include it. This is a tooling issue to be aware of - Droid-Shield may block commits of files containing test passwords.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff.discoveredIssues: 'Test file (test_auth.py) not committed due to Droid-Shield false positive on test passwords like Test1234'. The test file exists at apps/api/tests/test_auth.py but git status shows it as untracked.

summary: auth-email-otp feature implementation is complete and correct. All 8 expectedBehavior items are properly implemented with appropriate validation, error handling, and security measures. JWT access/refresh token rotation with reuse detection is correctly implemented. Rate limiting for login (5/min) and OTP (3/hour/phone) is in place. The logout response format was subsequently fixed in foundation-fix-2. No blocking issues found. Minor non-blocking issues: unused db dependency in get_current_user, in-memory rate limiting (should use Redis for production multi-instance deployments).
